route.name: Lost Sierra Loop
route.description: Official/public GPX route with forest roads, singletrack, pavement connectors, and mountain-town resupply.
route.surfaceMix: Official/public GPX route with forest roads, singletrack, pavement connectors, and mountain-town resupply.
route.bestSeason: Summer to early fall after snowmelt; refresh fire, smoke, and Plumas NF alerts before departure.

difficultyProfile.surface: Official/public GPX route with forest roads, singletrack, pavement connectors, and mountain-town resupply.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Town services and mountain water sources exist, but final water and campground status must be refreshed.
resources.food: Quincy and Taylorsville are key service anchors; confirm hours.
resources.camping: Quincy/Taylorsville/Graeagle-area camp or lodging sequence must be locked for July 10-13.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Use the official GPX as the line of record.
Fri-Sun is more realistic than pure Sat-Sun.
Refresh Plumas NF alerts and camp/lodging before promotion.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Fire/smoke/campground refresh still required for Plumas NF.

Lost Sierra Loop lacks a verified July 10-13 camp/lodging sequence and final Plumas fire/weather/smoke refresh.

Lost Sierra Loop needs an exact 2-day versus 3-day itinerary before next-weekend promotion.
No verified July 10-13 camp or lodging sequence is recorded.
Plumas National Forest high fire danger and departure-week smoke/weather remain unresolved.
Mid-summer/fall water may require carrying more water; longest gaps are not measured in the workspace.
Route skirts private property between Quincy and Sloat; rider must stay on the GPX and legal roads.
Lost Sierra Loop has no locked night-by-night camp/lodging sequence for July 10-13.
Exact July 10/11/12 overnight availability is not publicly proven.
Taylorsville Campground is first-come, first-served in the route source, so it cannot clear a date-specific availability gate from public sources alone.
Primitive Spanish Creek/dispersed options do not provide public date-specific availability proof.
Plumas NF fire danger is High, and the July 2 official release says fire restrictions are expected within the next couple weeks.
Smoke/weather and final fire-restriction status need a July 8-10 refresh.
Midsummer water reliability is uncertain; exact water status and longest dry carry are not fully cleared.
requiredFixes: Refresh Plumas NF alerts/fire/smoke and Quincy/Taylorsville services.
Lock camp/lodging sequence for July 10-13.
Build a night-by-night Quincy/Taylorsville/Graeagle camp or lodging plan and verify availability.
Measure water/resupply gaps from the GPX and verify all stores/campground water.
Refresh Plumas fire restrictions and smoke/forecast within 24-48 hours of departure.
Confirm Taylorsville campground/open services or exact lodging/camp alternate.
Choose 2-day or 3-day itinerary and measure water gaps.
Refresh Plumas fire/smoke/weather within 24-48 hours of departure.
Lock the night-by-night plan: Portola/Taylorsville/Quincy/Graeagle or legal dispersed camp options.
Verify exact lodging/camp availability and water at the chosen overnight anchors.
Measure the longest GPX water gap and mark filter/carry points.
Refresh Plumas alerts, fire restrictions, smoke/AQI, and weather on July 8-10.
Call or confirm Taylorsville Campground operations, or choose a reservable/lodging alternate and verify exact July 10-13 availability.

Replace first-come/primitive overnight assumptions with reservable campgrounds or lodging that publicly shows availability for July 10, July 11, and July 12, or obtain direct operator confirmation and record it.
Lock one exact itinerary: 2-day, 3-day, or 4-day, with nightly anchors and bailouts.
Refresh Plumas NF alerts, fire restrictions, InciWeb, AirNow smoke, and NWS forecast within 24-48 hours of departure.
Confirm current water availability at Taylorsville, Quincy, Redbridge/Spanish Creek, Sloat/Long Valley Creek, and GPX tank/creek waypoints.
Audit any private-property-adjacent or trail segments against current official maps/orders before promotion.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: What changed in the July 9-10 final refresh for closures, weather, smoke, water, and camps?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: 111 miles over 2-3 days is plausible only for a strong rider after an exact itinerary choice.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Public BIKEPACKING.com route page exposes a Download GPX control, and the local imported GPX is Lost Sierra Loop v2.0 from the public v2025.09 GPX.
promotionChecklist.campingPermitsNote: No exact public proof was found for July 10, July 11, and July 12 overnight availability. Taylorsville is first-come, and Spanish Creek/dispersed and Redbridge options do not publicly prove date-specific capacity.
promotionChecklist.closuresFireWeatherNote: Smoke, fire restrictions, and weather cannot be cleared for July 10-13 on July 2. Plumas NF says fire restrictions are expected within the next couple weeks.
promotionChecklist.legalAccessNote: Plumas NF official alerts page currently shows no featured alerts and High fire danger plus a camping stay/occupancy order, but this does not prove every GPX road/trail segment is open and bike-legal.
promotionChecklist.waterResupplyNote: Route source and GPX support towns/services at Portola, Taylorsville, Quincy, and water waypoints, but midsummer creek reliability is uncertain and exact July water status is not confirmed.
promotionChecklist.mapArtifactsNote: Public GPX and terrain/topographic map artifacts are present.

days.name: Lost Sierra Loop segment 1
days.camp: Quincy/Taylorsville/Graeagle-area camp or lodging sequence must be locked for July 10-13.

waypoints.name: End of the nasty climb. Whew!
Steep, loose, and exposed grade ahead!
The last couple of miles are on the highway.
Great views with Mount Lassen in the distance.
Where the Full Hough and Partial Hough options merge.
Official start of the Mount Hough trail

readinessEvidence.finding: Official page lists no featured alerts at this time and fire danger status High for Plumas National Forest.
Plumas says all fireworks are prohibited, the forest is not yet in fire restrictions, and fire restrictions are expected within the next couple weeks.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Plumas National Forest July 2 wildfire prevention release

readinessEvidence.finding: Plumas says Red Feather Campground opened for the season, while Little Beaver and Running Deer remain closed this season due to water-system issues; exact route camp sequence still needs verification.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Plumas National Forest June 26 recreation site release

readinessEvidence.finding: Plumas warns recreationists to be prepared for variable weather, cold water, heat/cold illness risk, and increasing recreation incidents.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Plumas National Forest June 26 recreation site release

readinessEvidence.finding: Subagent reported public GPX, terrain map, and current Plumas screen pass, with high fire danger and T-minus smoke/weather still pending.
